channel.title: Alejandro W. Cuatrecasas Schmitz
channel.description: Recent content on Alejandro W. Cuatrecasas Schmitz

item.title: Too Small To Fail
item.description:
The second day my wife and I arrived in Raleigh, we saw it painted on a wall. Four words. We stopped. We looked at each other. We didn’t need to say anything.

Too small to fail.

I’ve been carrying those words since. This is my attempt to explain why.

BTW — I’m writing here for the same reason I do most things: I need structure to understand, and I need to understand to remember. This is my corner — outside my head — to shape thoughts, ask questions, and share things worth the time.

item.title: About
item.description:
I’m relentlessly curious — from science to the meaning of life. Obsessed with spending time on what matters. I chase happiness, and try to bring it to others.

I often think about the infinity of the universe and the tininess of our presence in it. It keeps things in perspective.

I believe in having clear goals. In simplicity as a form of excellence. And in being a good person as a non-negotiable.

item.title: Good stuff
item.description: Work in progress — a living list of things worth your time.

item.title: Questions
item.description: Work in progress — things I keep asking myself.
